narrative: Dear Alex, imagine the admissions team looking at your application like they're piecing together the story of your journey to the bar exam, drawing from patterns they've seen in thousands of students just like you. Your Year 3 grades placing you in the 5th decile stood out as the biggest chapter pulling the prediction toward a tougher road ahead, suggesting that mid-pack performance late in law school often signals real hurdles with the bar's intensity. Right behind that, your solid 8th decile in Year 1 hinted at a pattern of not quite surging ahead, which weighed heavily in the same direction. Coming from a lower-middle family income added another layer, as the model sees this background sometimes stretching resources thin for the grueling prep needed to pass. Your Asian background played a role too, fitting into trends where high expectations can sometimes clash with bar exam pressures. With an LSAT of 21 and undergrad GPA of 3.1, those scores painted a picture of steady but not standout academic firepower, nudging the odds further toward the 41% failure risk. Your full-time status pulled in that direction as well, reflecting how diving straight into practice without a breather can dilute focus on exam mastery. On a brighter note, being male tipped things slightly toward success in the model's view. Even with your clear aim to target law school, it was this combination—especially those deciles and income—that built the overall narrative of higher risk, leading to the tough call on your application. It's not the end of your story, though; many rewrite these predictions with targeted steps like extra bar prep or grade boosts. You've got strengths to build on—keep pushing.
